title: Too Sweet
lyrics:
[Verse 1]
It can't be said I'm an early bird
  > [#1, 43 votes] Setting the scene for the couples juxtaposing personalities, the narrator stays out late, waking up very grumpy unlike his counterpart
It's 10 o'clock before I say a word
Baby, I can never tell
  > [#2, 20 votes] The narrator is expressing that he fundamentally doesn’t understand how she’s capable of living her life so innocently, as he finds it impossible. He’s envious of her ability to do so, but knows he...
How do you sleep so well?
You keep tellin' me to live right
To go to bed before the daylight
  > [#3, 33 votes] The partner is trying to help him. Trying to start a healthier routine, get them on the same timeline
But then you wake up for the sunrise
  > [#4, 19 votes] On the previous line, his lover tells him he needs to sleep earlier, go to bed at a regular time. Yet, they wake right at sunrise, which could be hypocrisy, but paired with the next line (“you don’t...
You know you don't gotta pretend
  > [#5, 26 votes] The narrator believes his partner’s habits (going to bed/waking up early, eating healthy) to be a farce put up for others, most likely due to the severity they take it to. The narrator loves this...
Baby, now and then
  > [#6, 44 votes] Here, he poses the question to his partner asking if they’ve never had the urge to be less disciplined and possibly stating examples of his own sleeping habits indicating that he goes out late at...

[Pre-Chorus]
Don't you just wanna wake up, dark as a lake
Smellin' like a bonfire, lost in a haze?
If you're drunk on life, babe, I think it's great
But while in this world

[Chorus]
I think I'll take my whiskey neat
  > [#7, 74 votes] Neat whiskey and black coffee are the “straight” unaltered versions of their respective beverages, and are generally seen as less pleasant than altered or sweetened versions. This points to the idea...
My coffee black and my bed at 3
  > [#8, 41 votes] A reference to a ‘stay out late’, ‘live it up’ lifestyle. While she’s getting to bed at a ‘decent time’ to live right and get up early, he’s burning the candle at both ends and living hard/wild at...
You're too sweet for me
You're too sweet for me
  > [#9, 16 votes] It should be noted that the views of the persona in this song should not be excessively conflated with Hozier’s own. Hozier has a tendency to write from the perspective of unreliable narrators and to...
I take my whiskеy neat
My coffee black and my bed at 3
  > [#10, 10 votes] He drinks black coffee, or coffee with no sweetener or milk. This coffee has a very bitter taste. Many prefer sweetener in their coffee. “… my bed at three” shows that he stays up late.
You're too sweet for mе
You're too sweet for me
  > [#11, 19 votes] In the back round of these two lyrics there’s wedding bells, while early on theorized to be an indication that he still wants her despite all this. With the context of the song it becomes clear that...

[Post-Chorus]
Ooh, ooh
Ooh, ooh

[Verse 2]
I aim low
I aim true, and the ground's where I go
  > [#12, 23 votes] This is a play on the term “going to ground”. Hozier is saying that he goes to work to hide away and be left alone. This also allows him to use the earth and dirt imagery from Unreal, Unearth.
I work late where I'm free from the phone
And the job gets done
But you worry some, I know
But who wants to live forever, babe?
You treat your mouth as if it's Heaven's gate
The rest of you like you're the TSA
  > [#13, 77 votes] The Travel Security Agency heavily regulates liquids taken through airport security. Hozier is saying the subject of the song monitors and measures everything they put in their body, pursuing a...
I wish I could go along
Babe, don't get me wrong

[Pre-Chorus]
You know you're bright as the morning, as soft as the rain
  > [#14, 34 votes] Serves as a double etendre: while Hozier is referring to the subject as being a bright and cheery person, the use of the word “morning” alludes to its homonym “mourning”. The chorus makes use of a...
Pretty as a vine, as sweet as a grape
  > [#15, 30 votes] Hozier is acknowledging that the subject has potential to become the kind of person he desires. Sunlight, water, the vine and the grape, all leading up to wine. He sees that they can become this...
If you can sit in a barrel, maybe I'll wait
  > [#16, 67 votes] whiskey and wine are both barrel-aged, fermented over years to create alcoholic and bitter results. Hozier/speaker would prefer the subject more jaded– as they are, like un-fermented grapes– they are...
Until that day
  > [#17, 13 votes] The last three lines of this verse show exactly how Hozier is feeling about this girl. He compares her to wine and tells her to mature and become bitter in a barrel, which we all know is pointless if...

[Chorus]
I'd rather take my whiskey neat
My coffee black and my bed at 3
You're too sweet for me
You're too sweet for me
I take my whiskey neat
My coffee black and my bed at 3
You're too sweet for me
You're too sweet for me

[Post-Chorus]
Woah, woah
Woah, woah
Woah, woah
Woah, woah

[Outro]
I take my whiskey neat
My coffee black and my bed at 3
You're too sweet for me
You're too sweet for me
